_description: Per-sign scholarly metadata. Keys are Barthel code strings. Keys starting with '_' are schema metadata.
_note: Populate from Horley (2021) and Fischer (1997). The 'is_taxogram_candidate' field for sign 200 is consensus; all others require sign_classifier.py to compute from corpus statistics.

_fields.known_as: string — informal name if one exists
_fields.scholarly_readings: list[string] — proposed phoneme/morpheme readings from literature
_fields.is_taxogram_candidate: bool — true if scholarly consensus flags this as a taxogram
_fields.frequency_note: string — qualitative frequency note from Barthel (1958)

001.notes: One of the most frequent signs in the corpus.

200.frequency_note: Very high frequency; appears in virtually all tablets.
200.notes: The primary rongorongo taxogram (Horley 2021, §3.2). Marks the boundary between syntactic units. Must be retained and tagged rather than silently stripped — see parallels.py tag_taxogram_positions().
